title: Price ceilings and floors
description: An exercise providing students with the opportunity to demonstrate their understanding of government price controls.

assessment_items.question_content:
Governments use various measures to influence prices and market outcomes.

**Which of the following best describes a government price control?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a government-imposed limit on the amount of a good that can be produced
a government-imposed minimum or maximum price for a good or service
a government policy that promotes competition among businesses
a government tax on goods that are considered harmful to public health

assessment_items.question_content:
Governments can use price ceilings to influence prices.

**Which of the following best describes a price ceiling?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a government mandated policy that sets the maximum price allowed to be charged
a government-mandated minimum price that must be paid for a good or service
the maximum amount of goods or services a company is allowed to produce
a government policy that ensures prices are always at their market equilibrium level

assessment_items.question_content:
Governments can use price floors to influence prices.

**Which of the following best describes a price floor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a  government-imposed minimum price that must be paid for a good or service
a government-imposed limit on how high the price of a product or service can go
a government policy that sets the price of a good or service at the lowest possible market level
a government limit on the maximum quantity of a product that can be sold in the market

assessment_items.question_content:
A city council is considering implementing a price control on rental housing in their city. 

**What is the council's primary purpose for implementing a price control?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to ensure that landlords can maximize their profits
to stabilize the rental market by eliminating competition
to protect tenants from excessively high rents and ensure affordability
to encourage landlords to supply more rental homes

assessment_items.question_content:
The City of Richmond is looking to implement rent control in a form of a price ceiling. 

**What is the primary purpose of implementing rent control?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to make housing more affordable for low- and middle-income tenants
to encourage landlords to invest in more rental properties
to increase the overall supply of rental housing in the market
to allow tenants to rent properties at below-market rates indefinitely

assessment_items.question_content:
In the State of Virginia, government leaders are looking to implement a state minimum wage as a form of price floor. 

**What is the primary purpose of implementing a minimum wage in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to ensure workers receive a wage that meets basic living standards
to guarantee full employment across the economy
to increase overall productivity in the workplace
to prevent inflation by controlling wage levels

assessment_items.question_content:
The government has imposed a price ceiling on essential medications to make them more affordable. 

**What is the unintended consequence of this policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand and supply for medications reach an equilibrium.
The quality of medications improves due to increased competition among suppliers.
There is a shortage of these medications in the market.
People's need for these essential medications is fully met due to their lower prices.

assessment_items.question_content:
A local government imposes a price ceiling on rent, setting it below the market rate.

**What will most likely happen in the rental market as a result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: More rental units will become available as landlords try to attract tenants.
Landlords will invest more in property maintenance to attract renters.
Tenants will find it easier to secure rental housing due to lower prices.
A shortage of rental units will develop as demand increases and supply decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shows the market supply and demand for bread with a government-imposed price control.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Based on the graph, identify the type of price control, and what type of disequilibrium that price control creates.**

A [[☃ dropdown 1]] is causing a [[☃ dropdown 2]] of bread because the price is set [[☃ dropdown 3]] the equilibrium level.
hints: The equilibrium dot on the graph marks where supply and demand for bread meet. This indicates what the bread would cost without price controls. The dashed line represents the government-mandated price control.
The dashed line on the graph is set *below* the equilibrium, and it indicates the price *ceiling* for the cost of bread. The price of bread cannot exceed this line.
This price ceiling leads to a shortage of bread, as the quantity demanded exceeds the quantity supplied at the controlled price.
A $\boxed{\text{price ceiling}}$ is  causing a $\boxed{\text{shortage}}$ of bread because the price is set $\boxed{\text{below}}$ the equilibrium level.

choices.content: price ceiling
price floor
shortage
surplus
above
below

options.alt: A graph titled "Market with Price Control" with the x-axis labeled "Quantity" and the y-axis labeled "Price". The graph features a blue upward-sloping supply curve labeled "Supply " and a red downward-sloping demand curve labeled "Demand (D)". The curves intersect at the equilibrium point, which is marked with a black dot and labeled "Equilibrium". A dashed line below the equilibrium price represents a price control. The price control line is shown below the equilibrium price, indicating that the price is capped below the level where supply equals demand.
options.caption: Market with Price Control

assessment_items.question_content:
A government has set a price floor on wheat at $\$15$ per bushel to support local farmers' income. 

**What could be a possible implication of this policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for wheat increases, leading to a surplus in the market.
The supply of wheat decreases as farmers are encouraged to produce more expensive crops.
The supply of wheat increases, potentially leading to a surplus in the market.
The demand and supply for wheat remain the same because the price floor doesn't affect the market.

assessment_items.question_content:
A government sets a price floor on wages, ensuring that workers cannot be paid less than a certain hourly rate. 

**What is the most likely outcome in the labor market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All workers will find jobs because employers must pay higher wages.
Employers will hire more workers because of the increased wage.
A surplus of labor (unemployment) will develop as the supply of workers exceeds the demand.
The market will adjust, and unemployment will decrease as wages rise.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shows the market supply and demand for labor. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the graph, identify the type of price control, and what type of disequilibrium that price control creates.**

The price control is a [[☃ dropdown 1]], causing a [[☃ dropdown 2]] because the price is set [[☃ dropdown 3]] the equilibrium level.
hints: The equilibrium price point on the graph is where supply and demand for labor meet in the market.  This indicates a price for labor that the market supports without a price control. The dashed line represents the government-mandated price control.
The dashed line on the graph is set *above* the equilibrium, and it indicates the price *floor* for the cost of labor. The price of labor cannot fall below this line.
The price floor leads to a surplus in quantity of labor because there are more people willing to work for the higher price.
The price control is $\boxed{\text{price floor}}$ causing a $\boxed{\text{surplus}}$ because the price is set $\boxed{\text{above}}$ the equilibrium level.

choices.content: price floor
price ceiling
shortage
surplus
above
below

options.alt: A graph titled "Market with Price Control" with the x-axis labeled "Quantity" and the y-axis labeled "Price". The graph features a blue upward-sloping supply curve labeled "Supply (S)" and a red downward-sloping demand curve labeled "Demand (D)". The curves intersect at the equilibrium point, which is marked with a black dot and labeled "Equilibrium". A dashed line above the equilibrium price represents a price control. The price control line is shown above the equilibrium price, indicating that the price is set above the level where supply equals demand.
options.caption: Market with Price Control
